67dec809e4a8.title: Get your menu online — 77% of diners check before visiting
67dec809e4a8.description: No online menu found. Most diners check the menu before committing. Without one, you lose the customer at the decision point.
67dec809e4a8.management_implication: Every day without a menu is a percentage of potential customers lost.

095d1a714a54.title: Close the hygiene gap — 4 to 5
095d1a714a54.description: An FSA rating of 4 is visible on your Google listing and the FSA website. Customers see it before they see your menu. Address the inspector's specific findings.
095d1a714a54.expected_upside: Remove the trust ceiling on your overall score

85841a6198ea.title: Establish basic web presence — you don't exist online beyond Google
85841a6198ea.description: No website, Facebook, or Instagram detected. Even a basic page improves discoverability and gives customers a reason to trust you before visiting.

a53684dd5e04.title: Fix the digital shopfront — you're losing walk-ins
a53684dd5e04.description: You deliver a strong experience but your online presence doesn't make it easy to act on. Can a new customer confirm your hours, see your menu, and book — all within 60 seconds on their phone? If not, that's your fix list.
a53684dd5e04.management_implication: Interest without conversion is vanity traffic.

9e12cd3d530a.title: Ranked #6 of 7 locally — peers are ahead
9e12cd3d530a.description: At percentile 16.7, customers in this area have higher-rated alternatives. This isn't a crisis but it means you're competing on convenience rather than reputation.

7a6b27f8d6cc.title: Guest experience trailing — investigate what peers do differently
7a6b27f8d6cc.description: You trail peers by 1.0 points on experience. This isn't a label problem — it means customers are getting a measurably better experience on this dimension from your competitors.
